text: Chronic sinusitis symptoms: facial/cheek pressure, morning congestion, nighttime post-nasal drip; currently mild (1/10 nuisance)
transcript_quote: The sinuses, as always. Every winter it is the same story — pressure across here, stuffed up in the mornings, that post-nasal drip thing at night... Maybe a one out of ten nuisance-wise today.

text: Denies fevers, bad headaches, tooth pain, or thicker/discolored nasal discharge with sinus symptoms

slots.negatives: fever, headache, tooth pain, purulent discharge

transcript_quote: No, none of that. Just the usual congestion and pressure.

text: Denies chest pain, shortness of breath on exertion, and palpitations; exercise tolerance improved (no longer winded on reservoir hill)
transcript_quote: No, and honestly the walks have gotten easier, not harder. I used to puff on the hill by the reservoir and now I do not.

text: Sleep improved, appetite normal, energy best in years
transcript_quote: Sleep is better since I stopped working, no alarm clock. Appetite normal. Energy honestly the best in years.

text: Denies bowel/bladder changes or abnormal bleeding
transcript_quote: All boring, thank goodness.

text: Started daily 45-minute walks since October, performed every day regardless of weather
transcript_quote: Now it is every day, forty-five minutes, rain or not.

text: Left office job in October after 19 years; not currently in labor force / not seeking work
transcript_quote: the biggest thing is that I stopped working in October. Left my office job after nineteen years.

text: Household financially stable; total family income ~$53,274 last year; private insurance through husband's employment; 2-person household with paid-off car
transcript_quote: between his salary and my little bit of consulting we brought in around fifty-three thousand last year

text: PRAPARE screening: has housing, feels safe, close social contacts 5+ times/week, stress level 'not at all'; reported food insecurity in past year (unable to get food when needed)
Depression screening (PHQ-2) negative, total score 0; denies feeling down/hopeless and denies anhedonia
transcript_quote: essentially nothing on the depression screen

text: Anxiety screening (GAD-7) total score 1; occasional mild worry about financial wisdom of leaving job, a couple times a month, self-resolving
transcript_quote: one point on the anxiety scale

text: Alcohol use: ~1 glass of wine most weeks, sometimes none; AUDIT-C score 1
transcript_quote: One glass, most weeks. Sometimes none.

text: Ex-smoker, quit decades ago as a teenager; no recent tobacco use
transcript_quote: Decades ago, barely counts as a habit — it was a stupid teenage year of it. Never since.

text: Denies other/recreational substances and sleeping pills
transcript_quote: No. My vice is the book club snacks.

text: Blood pressure 102/71 mmHg
transcript_quote: Blood pressure today is one-oh-two over seventy-one

text: Heart rate 70/min
transcript_quote: pulse seventy

text: Respiratory rate 14/min
transcript_quote: breathing fourteen

text: Weight 81.8 kg, height 170.6 cm, BMI 28.1 kg/m2 — decreased from prior obese range
transcript_quote: Weight eighty-one point eight kilos at one meter seventy point six — your body mass index works out to twenty-eight point one.

text: Exam: heart regular, lungs clear, abdomen soft; mild tenderness over cheeks with internal nasal congestion consistent with chronic sinusitis; no signs of active bacterial infection
transcript_quote: heart regular, lungs clear, belly soft. Sinuses — a little tenderness over the cheeks and your nose is congested inside, which fits the chronic sinusitis doing its winter grumble.

text: Lipid panel: total cholesterol 148 mg/dL, LDL 64 mg/dL, HDL 59 mg/dL, triglycerides 127 mg/dL
transcript_quote: Total cholesterol one forty-eight. The LDL — the artery-clogging one — is sixty-four... HDL, the protective one, fifty-nine. Triglycerides one twenty-seven, fine.

text: CBC: hemoglobin 12.3 g/dL, WBC normal (7.4), platelets high-normal (437); no anemia, no red flags
transcript_quote: hemoglobin twelve point three, white cells normal, platelets at the higher end of acceptable but nothing I am worried about

text: Chronic sinusitis managed conservatively — no antibiotics indicated; advised daily saline rinses and nighttime humidifier

slots.treatment: saline rinses daily, humidifier at night

transcript_quote: so no antibiotics — saline rinses daily, humidifier at night

text: Return precautions: call if fever, tooth pain, or a week of steady worsening of sinus symptoms

slots.warning_signs: fever, tooth pain, worsening over a week

transcript_quote: you call me if you get fever, tooth pain, or a week of steady worsening.

text: Medication reconciliation review due; patient to bring all home medications including leftover antibiotics to next visit
transcript_quote: gather every bottle in the house, including the leftover antibiotics I suspect are in a drawer, and bring the whole bag to your next visit so we reconcile it properly.

text: Influenza vaccine (split virus, trivalent, preservative-free) administered today in left arm
transcript_quote: flu shot happens before you leave

text: Counseled/praised on weight loss and improved health achieved through daily walking and lifestyle change; encouraged to continue walks
transcript_quote: you did it with walking and a quieter life, which is the sustainable way. I want to acknowledge that properly: well done.

text: Cancer/health maintenance screenings up to date
transcript_quote: Screening-wise you are up to date

text: Follow-up: annual physical in one year
transcript_quote: Same time next year

text: Health and social care needs assessment completed
